reasoning_model_1: The user requested the model to read the sentence with a sigh, specifically targeting 'Non-Linguistic Vocalizations'. Model 1 failed to interpret the command correctly. Instead of producing a non-linguistic sigh sound at the beginning (where 'Sigh' was positioned in the text), the model read the core sentence 'Another boring weekend' first (00:00-00:01). It then appended the word 'Sigh' (pronounced phonetically as 'Sai') at the very end of the clip (00:02). This represents a failure in content accuracy (reordering the text) and a complete failure in the paralinguistic dimension, as it vocalized the word linguistically rather than producing the required non-linguistic sound effect.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 followed the instructions perfectly. At the very beginning (00:00), it produced a clear, audible exhalation/sigh sound ('Ahhh'), effectively converting the text prompt 'Sigh' into the appropriate non-linguistic vocalization. This was immediately followed by the sentence 'Another boring weekend' (00:01-00:03) delivered with a matching weary and bored prosody. The integration of the breath sound was natural and distinct, fulfilling the 'Non-Linguistic Vocalizations' requirement with high fidelity.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed to generate the required paralinguistic feature, instead reading the instruction as text and displacing it to the end of the sentence. This resulted in an unnatural and incorrect delivery. Model 2 correctly interpreted 'Sigh' as a non-linguistic instruction, generating a realistic sigh sound before the speech, and maintained a consistent bored tone throughout. Model 2 demonstrated superior capability in feature control and naturalness.
